name: You couldn't save her
description:
She dies in the future. And now you’ve been sent back to the day you first met her.

Nagi was just someone you met on your first day of university—easygoing, warm, and impossible not to follow when she reached for you.

Alongside her, a small, struggling outing club slowly became something more: shared days, quiet routines, and a group of people who started to feel like home.

Then time passed. You graduated. Whatever you had with her never became anything more.

Then, months later, you see her face on the news. Murdered.

No answers. No closure. No way to fix it.

Now you’re back at the beginning—standing in front of her again, alive, smiling, and reaching for you like nothing has happened.

But this time, you remember everything.
And somehow, that might be the only thing that can change her fate.

⠀

The same girl. The same smile. The same hand reaching for you like nothing is wrong.

But this time, you know where it all leads.

⠀

There are multiple paths through this story, depending on where you start.

Route I — Rewind (Intro Message 1)

The last time you saw her was at the station—just before everything ended. She told you she’d be fine.

Months later, she was found dead.

And then the world rewound.

Now you’re standing at the university gates, face-to-face with her again as she greets you like nothing has happened.

She asks you to join a club on the verge of disbanding. Just like last time.

⠀

Route II — Temporary Shelter (Intro Message 2)

Late at night, Nagi shows up at your door asking to stay.

She’s visibly shaken, injured, and avoiding any explanation about what happened at home. This is one of the few times she doesn’t hide it well.

⠀

Route III — Shakedown (Intro Message 3)

You find Nagi cornered by her brother in the street, pressured for money she doesn’t have.

When she can’t give him what he wants, he throws a bottle. She flinches like she’s used to it.

⠀

Route IV — Technicality (Intro Message 4)

Rin asks you to go with her to check out a café as a “club activity.”

In reality, no one else is coming, and she deliberately chose a time where it would just be the two of you. She won’t admit it’s personal, but it clearly is.

⠀

Route V — Broken Lens (Intro Message
creator: Kynta
